exemplar.title: To what extent did Southwest Airlineâs holiday failure in 2022 affect its stakeholders?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did Southwest Airlineâs holiday failure in 2022 affect its stakeholders?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition of the key concept âchangeâ in the methodology section
Frequent and purposeful references to change throughout STEEPLE, Force Field analysis, and conclusion
weaknesses: Some descriptive passages (e.g., flight numbers, compensation figures) are not explicitly linked back to the change concept
Occasional generic statements about airlines weaken the Southwest-specific conceptual focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of the key concept with partially integrated analysis throughout (band 4). The concept of change guides most analytical sections, but its integration is not seamless in every paragraph, preventing a topâband (5) evaluation.

strengths: Five credible and diverse supporting documents meet the 3â5 requirement
Sources include government releases, major news outlets, and an academic paper, ensuring relevance
weaknesses: Depth is unevenâsome stakeholder impacts lack direct citation detail
Citation formatting relies on generic labels (âSupporting Document 1â) rather than standard authorâdate conventions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the documents are relevant and sufficiently inâdepth overall (band 3), uneven citation detail and formatting issues prevent awarding the top band (4) for fully rich and traceable evidence.

strengths: Appropriate selection of STEEPLE and Force Field frameworks for the research question
Clear application linking each factor to stakeholder impacts
weaknesses: Limited variety of analytical toolsâno financial ratios or alternative frameworks
Insufficient justification for why these tools were chosen and how force ratings were determined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The tools chosen are adequately applied and relevant (band 3), but the analysis lacks the consistent effectiveness and justification required for a higher band (4).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of quantitative data (e.g., cancelled flights, revenue loss, fines) to illustrate impacts
Adequate analysis linking STEEPLE factors to stakeholder consequences
weaknesses: Analysis is episodic with limited synthesis of interrelationships
Force Field diagram lacks numeric clarity and deeper evaluation of significance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission provides some effective analysis (band 3) with adequate data selection and evaluation, but it falls short of the sustained, thorough integration required for bands 4â5.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question and differentiates impacts across stakeholder groups
Conclusions are fully consistent with the presented evidence and include clear recommendations
weaknesses: Introduction of limitations in the conclusion could be better placed in a separate subsection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions fully address the research question and align with the data (band 3), meriting the maximum score for this criterion.

strengths: Logical, coherent structure with consistent headings and a comprehensive table of contents
Research question is prominently stated, guiding the reader
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers
Placement of limitations in the conclusion section could be reconsidered
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: An appropriate structure is maintained throughout (band 2), fulfilling the highest level of this criterion.

strengths: All required IB presentation elements are present (title page, word count, bibliography, labelled documents)
weaknesses: Reference formatting is inconsistent, with missing access dates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all core IB requirements (band 2), despite minor formatting inconsistencies.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is in place: Introduction â Methodology â Findings â Analysis tools â Conclusion, with a clear table of contents. Headings are used consistently, so the work demonstrates an **appropriate structure throughout** (highest available band for this strand).

The sources are credible and clearly relevant to the meltdown (major news outlets, government announcement, academic conference paper). They provide useful statistics and penalties, but depth is unevenâsome figures (e.g., stock fall) lack citation details and several stakeholder impacts rely on inference rather than source evidence. This fits **relevant and sufficiently in-depth** but not the full range and richness implied by the top band.

All IB-required elements are present: title page with word count and RQ, coherent sections, bibliography, and five labelled supporting documents. Therefore, the submission attains the top band for presentation.

Five supporting documents are listed and referenced (DOT press release, NYT, Reuters, Financial Times, ISCAP paper). This meets the rubric requirement of **3â5 documents** for the highest band on this strand.

Application is generally clear: each STEEPLE factor is linked to specific stakeholder effects, and the Force Field diagram quantifies driving/restraining forces. Nevertheless, large sections remain descriptive, and numerical tools are not integrated to test claims, so the relevance is **adequate but not consistently effective**, aligning with band 3.

Data such as 14,042 cancelled flights, $1.1 billion lost revenue and $140 million fine are used correctly to illustrate impact. Coverage across stakeholder groups is acceptable, but the dataset is narrow and largely secondary; no triangulation or primary data selection is attempted. This represents **adequate selection and use** (band 3).

The analysis moves beyond description by explaining how each STEEPLE factor harms or pressures stakeholders and weighing driving vs. restraining forces. Yet evaluation is episodic, with limited synthesis and little discussion of relative magnitude or alternative interpretations. This constitutes **some effective analysis** but not the âmostly integratedâ depth of band 4.

The student explicitly answers the research questionââstakeholders were affected negatively to a great extentââand differentiates between groups (customers, investors, regulators). This meets the criterion of **explicitly answering** the RQ (band 3).

The student selects STEEPLE and Force Field Analysisâboth appropriate to examine external pressures and stakeholder forcesâand supplements them with basic numerical evidence. The choices are **adequate** for the question, though limited in variety (no financial ratios, stakeholder mapping, or break-even tools), matching band 3.

The conclusion restates key numerical evidence (stock decline, compensation figures) and logically follows the preceding analysis, showing consistency. It directly judges the extent of stakeholder impact and recommends change, satisfying the descriptor for conclusions **fully consistent with evidence** (top band).

The IA maintains a clear, data-rich focus on **Southwest Airlines** at every stage: introduction, STEEPLE table, Force-Field diagram, cost figures and penalty amounts. The effects on customers, employees, regulators and investors are consistently linked back to Southwestâs operational systems and 2022 meltdown.

While the analysis is thorough, a few sections drift into generic statements about airlines or âbusinessesâ without detailed Southwest context, preventing full âthroughout assessmentâ integration. Hence the evidence supports a **strong but not exhaustive connection**, corresponding to band 4.

The student defines the concept of **change** in the methodology section and repeatedly frames the STEEPLE, Force-Field and conclusion around the need for organisational change at Southwest. Throughout the findings and analysis, phrases such as âthis drives changeâ and âpressure to alter strategyâ show that the concept is actively referenced, not merely stated.

However, the integration is not seamless across every paragraph; some descriptive passages (e.g., recounting flight numbers or compensation figures) are detached from the conceptual lens. Because the concept guides mostâbut not allâanalytical sections, the work aligns with the descriptor for a **strong understanding with partially integrated analysis** rather than the fully embedded approach required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly and prominently stated, providing a solid foundation for the analysis.
While the key concept of âChangeâ is named, its relevance could be reinforced by briefly linking it to Southwestâs specific holiday failure context here.
The inclusion of word count and formatting elements meets IB presentation requirements.
Citations rely heavily on âSupporting Document 1â instead of author-date format, reducing academic rigor.
The methodology defines âchangeâ clearly with a subject-guide reference, establishing a strong conceptual anchor.
The findings section effectively presents quantitative data to illustrate stakeholder impact.
Include rationale for each forceâs rating, perhaps as footnotes or in an appendix, to reduce subjectivity.
The table of contents lists all sections comprehensively, aiding navigation and demonstrating clear organization.
Consider adding page numbers beside each entry to enhance usability and demonstrate attention to detail.
The introduction provides rich contextual background, but it remains mostly descriptive without linking data back to the key concept of change.
The methodology names STEEPLE and Force Field analyses but does not justify their selection criteria or relevance to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider outlining how secondary sources were chosen and any inclusion/exclusion criteria to enhance methodological transparency.
The narrative reports figures but stops short of evaluating their significance or trends over time.
Expanding beyond a single conference source for traveller statistics could strengthen data validity.
The STEEPLE table comprehensively addresses all seven factors and links them to stakeholder impacts.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Some analysis cells restate facts rather than exploring underlying causal relationships or legal nuances.
To deepen evaluation, integrate quantitative metrics (e.g., percentage stock impact) into the STEEPLE analysis.
The narrative on page 7 reiterates table content but offers limited synthesis of how factors interrelate.
Consider comparing relative magnitudes of social, economic, and political factors to guide prioritization of change.
The transition into the Force Field Analysis lacks an explanation of the rating methodology for driving and restraining forces.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Force Field Analysis tool is introduced effectively, demonstrating appropriate selection of frameworks.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Force Field diagram lacks numeric labels and a clear legend explaining the scale from 1â5.
Discussion of driving and restraining forces identifies valid contributors but omits explicit reference to diagram values.
Introducing limitations in the conclusion introduces new content; consider a separate limitations subsection earlier.
In-text citations could more explicitly link specific claims to their supporting documents to improve traceability.
To strengthen your evaluation, reference the cumulative driving versus restraining scores using $$...$$ notation.
The conclusion succinctly answers the research question and aligns with the presented evidence.
The bibliography includes diverse and credible sources, supporting a well-rounded investigation.
Reference formatting is inconsistent (e.g., missing access dates), which may detract from academic credibility.
Supporting documents list is complete and clearly referenced, meeting the 3â5 document requirement.
